samplerate.__description: Get the current audio device sample rate, as a number. The audio sample rate is measured in hertz (Hz) and it determines the sound quality
samplerate.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local function write_property(name, value)
    -- Fancy Formatting
    console.writecolor("brightwhite", name.." :\t")
    console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end
-- Display audio properties
write_property("Audio device", "\t"..audio.device)
write_property("Device samplerate", audio.samplerate)
write_property("Device channels", audio.channels)
samplerate.__tinydesc: Get the current device sample rate

devices.__description:
Get the list of detected audio devices, as a table, that contains the following fields :
  • "playback": to get a table that lists audio playing devices.
  • "recording": to get a table that lists audio recoding devices.
devices.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

print("Audio playback devices found :")
for device in each(audio.devices.playback) do
  print("\t|- "..device)
end

print("\nAudio recording devices found :")
for device in each(audio.devices.recording) do
  print("\t|- "..device)
end
devices.__tinydesc: Get the list of detected audio devices

maxdistance.__description: Get or set the maximum distance for attentation calculation. This property default value is set to 3.4028234663853e+38
maxdistance.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.maxdistance)
maxdistance.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound maximum distance for attenuation calculation

playing.__description: Check if the sound is playing, a boolean value. If the property is true, the sound is currently playing.
playing.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
sound:play()
-- Keep playing the music until it is finished
while sound.playing do 
  sleep()
end
playing.__tinydesc: Check if the sound is playing

doppler.__description: Get or set the sound doppler effect. This property default value is set to 1.0
doppler.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.doppler)

Sound.__description: The Sound object plays and controls audio with effects and spatial properties

mingain.__description: Get or set the minimum gain to apply from spatialization. This property default value is set to 0.0
mingain.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.mingain)
mingain.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound minimum gain from spatialization

pitch.__description: Get or set the sound pitch, number that controls the playback speed of the sound. A value of 1.0 means the sound will play at original speed.
pitch.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local ui = require "ui"
require "console"

local win = ui.Window("Music player", "single", 320, 200)

local file = sys.File("music.mp3")
win:status("Playing "..file.name)

local sound = audio.Sound(file)
sound.pitch = 1.5 -- Change the pitch of the audio
sound:play()

win:show()

while win.visible do 
    ui.update()
end

position.__description: Get or set the sound position in the world
position.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

local function printvector(v)
  -- Fancy Formatting
  console.writecolor("brightwhite", "Sound "..v..(#v < 7 and ":\t\t" or " :\t"))
  local value = "{ "
  for axis, val in pairs(sound[v]) do
    value = value..axis.." = "..val..", "
  end
  value = value:gsub(", $", " }")
  console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end

-- Get default listener position, direction, veolocity, worldup and cone
printvector("position")
printvector("direction")
printvector("velocity")
printvector("cone")
position.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound position in the world

rolloff.__description: Get or set the sound current rolloff (speed and direction)
rolloff.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.rolloff)

velocity.__description: Get or set the sound current velocity (speed and direction)
velocity.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

local function printvector(v)
  -- Fancy Formatting
  console.writecolor("brightwhite", "Sound "..v..(#v < 7 and ":\t\t" or " :\t"))
  local value = "{ "
  for axis, val in pairs(sound[v]) do
    value = value..axis.." = "..val..", "
  end
  value = value:gsub(", $", " }")
  console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end

-- Get default listener position, direction, veolocity, worldup and cone
printvector("position")
printvector("direction")
printvector("velocity")
printvector("cone")

pan.__description: Get or set the sound pan, number that controls the distribution of the sound in channels. A value of -1.0 means the sound is on the left channel, a value of -1.0 means the sound is on the right channel.
pan.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
print("Press a key to exit")

local p = 0
local delta = -0.1
sound.pan = p
sound:play()

-- Play the sound from left to right by changing sound.pan at each second
while (not console.keyhit) and sound.playing do
    p = p+delta
    sound.pan = p
    sleep(1000)
    if p <-0.5 then
        delta = 0.1 
    elseif p > 0.5 then
        delta = -0.1
    end
end

reverb.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Music player", "single", 320, 200)

local file = sys.File("music.mp3")
local ch = ui.Checkbox(win, "Reverb effect")
ch:center()

win:status("Playing "..file.name)

local sound = audio.Sound(file)
sound:play()

function ch:onClick()
    if ch.checked then
        sound:reverb() -- apply reverb effect with default values
    else
        sound:reverb(nil) -- suppress reverb effect
    end
end

win:show()

while win.visible do 
    ui.update()
end

__params.__description: A number representing the virtual room size: 0.0 is like a closet, 1.0 is like a large auditorium. The default value is 0.5
A number representing the absorption of high frequencies in the virtual room: 0.0 means no absorption, 1.0 means maximal absorption. The default value is 0.25
A number representing the "width" of the reverb effect : high values create a more "spacious" effect The default value is 1.0 second.
A number representing the volume adjustement of the original ("dry") sound. The default value is 0.0
A number representing the volume adjustement of the reverb ("wet") effect. The default value is 0.33333333333

reverb.__description: Apply a reverberation effect on the sound. Calling this method with an explicit nil will suppress the reverb effect.
reverb.__tinydesc: Apply a reverb effect to the sound

ended.__description: Check if the sound has ended, a boolean value. When the property is true, the end of the sound have been reached.
ended.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
sound:play()

repeat
  sleep()
until sound.ended -- Repeat until the sound has ended
ended.__tinydesc: Check if the sound has ended

seek.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

-- Loads and play a music
local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
sound:play()

-- plays the sound 2 sec...
sleep(2000)

-- ...then advance cursor position at 33.1 seconds
sound:seek(33.1)

while sound.playing do
  sleep()
end

__params.__description: A number representing the time position on the sound, in seconds.

stop.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

-- Loads and play a music
local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
sound:play()

-- will stop the sound in one minute (= 60000ms)
sound:stop(60000)

while sound.playing do
  sleep()
end

__params.__description: A number representing an optional delay before the sound is stopped, in milliseconds (defaulting to 0)

stop.__description: Plays the sound, from its current cursor position

loop.__description: Get or set the sound looping mode, a boolean value. If set to true, the sound will be played again from start once its end have been reached.
loop.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Music player", "fixed", 320, 200)
local file = sys.File("music.mp3")
local ch = ui.Checkbox(win, "Play in loop")
ch:center()

win:status("Playing "..file.name)

local sound = audio.Sound(file)
sound:play()

function ch:onClick()
    sound.loop = self.checked -- Toggle if the sound loops
end

win:show()

while win.visible do 
    ui.update()
end

duration.__description: Get the sound is length in seconds
duration.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
local time = sys.Datetime()
-- Calculate the length of the audio
time.hour = 0
time.minute = math.floor(sound.duration/60)
time.second = math.floor(sound.duration - time.minute*60)

print("music.mp3 length is "..time.time)
duration.__tinydesc: Get the sound length in seconds

maxgain.__description: Get or set the maximum gain to apply from spatialization. This property default value is set to 1.0
maxgain.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.maxgain)
maxgain.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound maximum gain from spatialization

volume.__description: Get or set the sound volume, a linear scale, with 0 results in silence and anything above 1 results in sound amplification.
volume.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

print("Playing some music")
print("Press UP and DOWN arrow keys to change sound volume")
print("Press SPACE to exit")

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

while true do 
    local c, special = console.readchar()
    if special == true then
        if c == "H" then        -- UP key have been pressed
            sound.volume = sound.volume + 0.1
            print("Sound volume set to "..sound.volume)
        elseif c == "J" then    -- DOWN key have been pressed
            sound.volume = sound.volume - 0.1
            print("Sound volume set to "..sound.volume)
        end
    elseif c == " " then        -- SPACE key have been pressed
        print("Stop playing...")
        break
    end
end

direction.__description: Get or set the sound direction in the world
direction.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

local function printvector(v)
  -- Fancy Formatting
  console.writecolor("brightwhite", "Sound "..v..(#v < 7 and ":\t\t" or " :\t"))
  local value = "{ "
  for axis, val in pairs(sound[v]) do
    value = value..axis.." = "..val..", "
  end
  value = value:gsub(", $", " }")
  console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end

-- Get default listener position, direction, veolocity, worldup and cone
printvector("position")
printvector("direction")
printvector("velocity")
printvector("cone")
direction.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound direction in the world

fade.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

-- Loads and play a music
local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

-- fade out the sound volume for 3 second
sound:fade(0, 1, 3000)

sound:play()
sleep(3000)

-- fade in the sound for 2 second
sound:fade(1, 0, 2000)

-- let the sound play until fade out effect is terminated
sleep(2000)

__params.__description: A number representing the starting volume. A value of 0.0 (the default) means silence. The special value -1 means the current volume
A number representing the ending volume. A value of 1.0 (the default) means maximum volume without amplification.
A number representing the duration of the fade effect in seconds. The default value is 1.0 second.

fade.__description: Gradual increase (fade out) or decrease (fade in) the volume of the sound over a period of time

cursor.__description: Get or set the sound cursor position in seconds, a value of 0.0 means the start of the sound.
cursor.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Music player", "fixed", 320, 200)
local file = sys.File("music.mp3")

local label = ui.Label(win, "sound.cursor = 0.0")
label.fontsize = 16
label:center();

local sound = audio.Sound(file)
sound:play()

win:show()

while win.visible do 
    label.text = "sound.cursor : "..string.format("%.1fs",sound.cursor)
    ui.update()
    sleep(100)
end
cursor.__tinydesc: Get/set the current sound cursor position

echo.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local ui = require "ui"

local win = ui.Window("Music player", "single", 320, 200)

local file = sys.File("music.mp3")
local ch = ui.Checkbox(win, "Echo effect")
ch:center()

win:status("Playing "..file.name)

local sound = audio.Sound(file)
sound:play()

function ch:onClick()
    if ch.checked then
        sound:echo(0.5, 0.4) -- apply an echo effect
    else
        sound:echo(nil) -- suppress the echo effect
    end
end

win:show()

while win.visible do 
    ui.update()
end

__params.__description:
A number representing the echo delay in seconds, the time delay between the original sound and its echo.
The default value is 0.4 (the default).

A number representing the echo delay in seconds, the time it takes for the echo to decay to zero after the sound has stopped emitting.
The default value is 0.3 (the default).
A number representing the volume adjustment of the original ("dry") sound. The default value is 1.0
A number representing the volume adjustment of the echo effect ("wet") sound. The default value is 1.0

echo.__description: Apply an echo effect on the sound. Calling this method with an explicit nil will suppress the echo effect.
echo.__tinydesc: Apply an echo effect to the sound

mindistance.__description: Get or set the minimum distance for attentation calculation. This property default value is set to 3.4028234663853e+38
mindistance.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

print(sound.mindistance)
mindistance.__tinydesc: Get/set the sound minimum distance for attenuation calculation

play.__returns: This function returns a Task, that will end when the sound will finish to play. You can cancel the play by calling Task.cancel() if needed.
play.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

-- loads a explosion sound
local sound = audio.Sound("boom.wav")
sound:play(1000) -- Start the audio from 1000ms
sleep((sound.duration+0.8)*1000)
console.writecolor("red", "BOOOUUUM !!!\n")

__params.__description: A number representing an optional delay before the sound is played, in milliseconds (defaulting to 0)

play.__description: Plays the sound, from its current cursor position

__params.__description: A File or a filename as a string representing the file to load the sound from.

A boolean value that prevent decoding of the binary sample when set to true.The sound will be decoded later (with a risk of performance hit). If set to false (the default value), the binary sample will be decoded during load time.

A boolean value that prevent using pitching/doppler effect on the sound when set to true (provides better optimization). If set to false (the default value), pitching and doppler effect will be available for this sound.

A boolean value that prevent to use spatialization on the sound when set to true (provides better optimization). If set to false (the default value), spatialization will be available for this sound.


The audio module support the following file types :
  • WAV (Waveform Audio file format) *.wav
  • MP3 (Moving Picture Experts Group Layer 3) *.mp3
  • FLAC (Free Lossless Audio Codec) *.flac
  • OGG VORBIS *.ogg

constructor.__description: The Sound constructor returns a Sound instance representing a binary sample data loaded from a file.
constructor.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")
sound:play()
-- play sound for 5sec and exit
sleep(5000)

cone.__description: Get or set the sound cone, that controls how sound is attenuated based on the Sound's direction
cone.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local sound = audio.Sound("music.mp3")

local function printvector(v)
  -- Fancy Formatting
  console.writecolor("brightwhite", "Sound "..v..(#v < 7 and ":\t\t" or " :\t"))
  local value = "{ "
  for axis, val in pairs(sound[v]) do
    value = value..axis.." = "..val..", "
  end
  value = value:gsub(", $", " }")
  console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end

-- Get default listener position, direction, veolocity, worldup and cone
printvector("position")
printvector("direction")
printvector("velocity") 
printvector("cone")

recdevice.__description: Get or set the current audio recording device, as a string
recdevice.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local function write_property(name, value)
    -- Fancy formatting
    console.writecolor("yellow", name..":\t")
    console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end
-- Display the audio properties
write_property("Audio recording device", audio.recdevice)
recdevice.__tinydesc: Get/set the current audio recording device

record.stop.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

io.write("Recording audio for 5 sec... ")

-- Start audio recording
audio.record.stop("sound.wav", "wav")

-- Wait for 5 sec during recording
sleep(5000)

-- Stops audio recording
audio.record.stop()
print("done !\n\nPlaying...")

-- Plays the recorded audio file
audio.Sound("sound.wav"):play():wait()

__description: The audio module plays and records audio using any audio device

play.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

-- loads a explosion sound
audio.play("boom.wav")
console.writecolor("red", "BOOOUUUM !!!\n")

__params.__description:
A File or a filename as a string representing the file to load the sound from.

This function provide the simplest but least flexible way to play a sound.
For more flexibility and possibilities, you can use a Sound object.

channels.__description:
Get the current audio device number of channels, as a number.
1 channel device will play sounds in "mono", while 2 channels device will play sounds in "stereo" and more channels will play sounds in "surround"
channels.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local function write_property(name, value)
    -- Fancy formatting
    console.writecolor("brightwhite", name.." :\t")
    console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end
-- Display the audio properties
write_property("Audio device", "\t"..audio.device)
write_property("Device samplerate", audio.samplerate)
write_property("Device channels", audio.channels)
channels.__tinydesc: Get the current device number of channels

playdevice.__description: Get or set the current audio playing device, as a string
playdevice.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

local function write_property(name, value)
    -- Fancy formatting
    console.writecolor("yellow", name..":\t")
    console.writecolor("cyan", value.."\n")
end
-- Display the audio properties
write_property("Audio playback device", audio.playdevice)
write_property("Device samplerate", audio.samplerate)
write_property("Device channels", audio.channels)
playdevice.__tinydesc: Get/set the current audio playing device

gain.__description: Get or set the master gain, the global loudness of sounds in decibels. This property provides global amplification of sounds.
gain.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

print("Playing some music")
print("Press UP and DOWN arrow keys to change gain")
print("Press SPACE to exit")
-- Play some music
audio.play("music.mp3")

while true do 
    local c, special = console.readchar()
    if special == true then
        if c == "H" then        -- UP key have been pressed
            audio.gain = audio.gain + 1
            print("Audio gain set to "..audio.gain)
        elseif c == "J" then    -- DOWN key have been pressed
            audio.gain = audio.gain - 1
            print("Audio gain set to "..audio.gain)
        end
    elseif c == " " then        -- SPACE key have been pressed
        print("Stop playing...")
        break
    end
end

volume.__description: Get or set the global master volume, a linear scale, with 0 results in silence and anything above 1 results in amplification.
volume.__example:
local audio = require "audio"
local console = require "console"

print("Playing some music")
print("Press UP and DOWN arrow keys to change volume")
print("Press SPACE to exit")

audio.play("music.mp3")

while true do 
    local c, special = console.readchar()
    if special == true then
        if c == "H" then        -- UP key have been pressed
            audio.volume = audio.volume + 0.5
            print("Audio volume set to "..audio.volume)
        elseif c == "J" then    -- DOWN key have been pressed
            audio.volume = audio.volume - 0.5
            print("Audio volume set to "..audio.volume)
        end
    elseif c == " " then        -- SPACE key have been pressed
        print("Stop playing...")
        break
    end
end

record.start.__returns: This function returns a boolean value, which indicates whether the recording session started successfully.
record.start.__example:
local audio = require "audio"

io.write("Recording audio for 5 sec... ")

-- Start audio recording
audio.record.start("sound.wav", "wav")

-- Wait for 5 sec during recording
sleep(5000)

-- Stops audio recording
audio.record.stop()
print("done !\n\nPlaying...")

-- Plays the recorded audio file
audio.Sound("sound.wav"):play():wait()

__params.__description: A File or a filename as a string representing the file to save audio data to.

An optional string that indicates the audio encoding format of the specified file :

  • "wav": audio format known for its high-quality sound and large file size (default).
  • "mp3": widely-used compressed audio format that balances good sound quality and small file size.
  • "aac": compressed audio format offering better sound quality than MP3 at similar bit rates.
An optional number indicating the number of channels that will be recorded, defaulting to 2.
An optional number indicating the number of channels that will be recorded, defaulting to 44100.

An optional number indicating the amount of data processed per unit of time in the audio file, defaulting to :

  • WAV format : 0
  • MP3 format : 128
  • AAC format : 96
